suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify in backend-worker runtime validation guidance that workers must preserve mission ownership boundaries and must not introduce backend process-management logic when the mission says to reuse an existing backend on port 8001.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: backend-worker SKILL.md step 4 says to verify contracts with curl on 8001, but does not remind workers to preserve mission ownership boundaries from AGENTS.md/environment.md. The failed prior review and the a557acbb handoff both describe this ambiguity around whether editing `.factory/services.yaml` to own backend start/stop is acceptable.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Document the canonical watchlist migration state markers and retry/finalization semantics (`legacy_migration_checked_at`, `legacy_migrated_at`, and `legacy_migration_completed_with_favorites`) so future workers understand when late-visible legacy favorites should still merge and when migrated legacy data must stay suppressed.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: app/services/favorites_service.py:243-299 implements the retry/finalization protocol; .factory/library/architecture.md documents canonical watchlist invariants but not these migration-state fields or their semantics.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Document the canonical watchlist migration state markers and retry/finalization semantics (`legacy_migration_checked_at`, `legacy_migrated_at`, and `legacy_migration_completed_with_favorites`) so future workers understand when late-visible legacy favorites should still merge and when migrated legacy data must stay suppressed.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: app/services/favorites_service.py:172-236 and 239-317 persist/read `legacy_migration_checked_at`, `legacy_migrated_at`, and `legacy_migration_completed_with_favorites`; .factory/library/architecture.md and .factory/library/contracts.md contain no migration-marker guidance.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Document the canonical watchlist migration state markers and retry/finalization semantics (`legacy_migration_checked_at`, `legacy_migrated_at`, and `legacy_migration_completed_with_favorites`) so future workers understand when late-visible legacy favorites should still merge and when migrated legacy data must stay suppressed.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: app/services/favorites_service.py:257-289 persists and reads `legacy_migration_completed_with_favorites`; AGENTS.md and .factory/library/architecture.md describe canonical migration invariants at a high level but do not document this marker or its retry/finalization semantics.
